spec_match.rationale: The probe now checks the actual Web Speech contract: processLocally, SpeechRecognition.available({ langs, processLocally, quality }), install() presence, local pack status, and permissions-policy state.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised Run Capability Probe, Reset, and all three Start/abort trial tiers; each control updated live UI state without console errors.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now matches the implemented behavior, including a brief recognition start/abort trial for command, dictation, and conversation tiers.

edge_cases.rationale: The page reports missing APIs, unavailable local packs, permission prompts, language-not-supported trial errors, and secure-context state without hiding failures.

references.rationale: The concept page links to ChromeStatus feature 5136859632107520, the Web Speech API available(options) spec section, and the Chrome 150 beta announcement.

design_system.rationale: The repaired controls use local classes with shared CSS variables, explicit button text/background colors, and no inline handlers or inline styles.

summary: Re-reviewed in chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148.0.0.0 at /v150/web-speech-api-on-device-recognition-quality/device-capability-probe/?verify=after-quality-trial-fix. The repaired probe returned quality statuses for command, dictation, and conversation via SpeechRecognition.available({ quality }); start/abort trials for all three tiers reported clean language-not-supported fallback states; Reset restored placeholder UI; conformance reported 6 PASS / 0 FAIL; console was empty and network requests for the page, shared CSS, fonts, and favicon completed successfully.
